file_analyses.file_purpose: YAML test case definition file for validating the Instant Services printer details modal functionality. This test case covers multiple test IDs (C31165177, C31266422, C30351306, C30351309, C30351310) and verifies the printer details modal display, content structure, HP Connected Account link functionality, and navigation flows within the HP Instant Ink dashboard overview page.

variables_and_constants.constant_value: Test Instant Services Printer details modal

test_case_metadata.description: Test Instant Services Printer details modal

steps.description: References external YAML file for login setup operations
References external YAML file for dashboard overview navigation

steps.prompt: Use test image for validating structural and contents on the page. Ignore the navigation pane of the Test image. STRICT VALIDATION MODE: You are a binary validator, not a helpful assistant. Return {"status": false} immediately if ANY condition fails. Do not make excuses or justifications. 1. Your focus area is after the navigation pane. 2. There must be exactly the word "Status" positioned at the top left. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 3. You should find HP printer model name below word "Status". If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 4. You should find printer readiness information and "Printer Details". If not, immediately return {"status": false}. Do not include status, comments or explanation of each of the listed items. If all conditions are met return {"status": true}
validation_points: HP printer model name below Status
steps.description: Verifies the status pane structure on the dashboard including Status header, printer model name, readiness information, and Printer Details link
Clicks on the Printer Details link to open the printer details modal

steps.prompt: Use test image for validating structural and contents on the page. Ignore the navigation pane of the Test image. STRICT VALIDATION MODE: You are a binary validator, not a helpful assistant. Return {"status": false} immediately if ANY condition fails. Do not make excuses or justifications. 1. Your focus area is on the modal. 2. There must be exactly the word "My Printer" positioned at the top left. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 3. You should find HP printer icon down below "My Printer". If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 4. You should find "HP Connected Account" hyperlink below the printer icon. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 5. You should find printer enrolled status below the HP connected account hyperlink. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. Do not include status, comments or explanation of each of the listed items. If all conditions are met return {"status": true}
validation_points: My Printer title at top left
steps.description: Verifies the left section of the printer details modal including My Printer title, printer icon, HP Connected Account link, and enrollment status

steps.prompt: Use test image for validating structural and contents on the page. Ignore the navigation pane of the Test image. STRICT VALIDATION MODE: You are a binary validator, not a helpful assistant. Return {"status": false} immediately if ANY condition fails. Do not make excuses or justifications. 1. Your focus area is on the modal. 2. You may optionally find "Printer Email" followed by email id. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 3. You should find "Printer Serial Number" followed by serial number. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 4. You should find "Service Enrolled Date" followed by date in month, date and year format. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 5. You should find "HP Connected Account" hyperlink below the printer icon. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 6. You should find printer enrolled status below the HP connected account hyperlink. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. Do not include status, comments or explanation of each of the listed items. If all conditions are met return {"status": true}
validation_points: Service Enrolled Date in month/date/year format

steps.label: HP Connected Account

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a comprehensive test case YAML file that validates the printer details modal functionality in the HP Instant Ink dashboard. The test follows a structured flow with setup, main steps, and cleanup phases. It uses AI-powered visual verification with strict validation prompts that check for specific UI elements and their positioning. The test includes multiple verification steps with screenshot capture, click actions for navigation, and escape key actions for closing modals and dismissing popups. The test references external YAML files for login setup and logout cleanup, promoting reusability. Notable features include the use of expand parameter for menu clicks, variable sleep times for different operations (ranging from 1 to 30 seconds), and detailed validation points in AI prompts that check for exact text positioning, presence of icons, hyperlinks, and data fields. The test covers both the left and right sections of the printer details modal separately, ensuring comprehensive validation of all modal content.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test case YAML file focuses on validating the HP Instant Ink navigation structure and content layout across multiple pages. It systematically navigates through all four main submenu items (Overview, Update Plan, Print and Payment History, Shipment Tracking) and verifies the layout and content of each page. The test uses a consistent pattern of click action followed by verification with AI-powered visual validation. Each verification step uses strict validation mode with binary pass/fail logic. The test includes variable wait times optimized for different page load scenarios (10s for dashboard, 30s for Overview, 20s for other pages, 3s for verifications). All pages share common UI elements like the instant ink logo and Select printer dropdown, which are validated on each page. The navigation pane verification is particularly important as it confirms the menu structure before navigating to individual pages. The test promotes reusability by referencing external YAML files for login and logout operations. Notable is the use of expand parameter when clicking HP Instant Ink to reveal the submenu structure.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test case YAML file focuses specifically on validating the plan details card component on the Overview page. The test is structured to verify the card in three stages: first the header and basic plan information, then the billing and shipping sections, and finally the action hyperlinks after scrolling. The use of vertical scroll (vscroll) action demonstrates the test's ability to handle content that extends beyond the initial viewport. The test validates both required fields (Overview title, instant ink logo, Plan Details, Subscription ID, price, page count, billing, shipping, and three action hyperlinks) and optional fields (remaining free months). The three action hyperlinks (Redeem Promo or PIN Code, Transfer Subscription to Another Printer, Cancel Instant Ink) represent key user actions available from the plan details card. The test uses consistent 3-second wait times for verifications and a 1-second wait after scroll. The validation prompts use strict binary validation mode ensuring any missing element results in immediate failure. The test references external YAML files for both login setup and dashboard overview navigation, promoting modularity and reusability.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test case YAML file focuses on validating the Overview page header section with particular emphasis on the printer selection and enrollment functionality. The test breaks down the header validation into two separate verification steps for better granularity and clearer failure diagnosis. The first verification checks the basic header structure (Overview title and instant ink logo), while the second verification checks the interactive components (Select printer dropdown with printer icon and model, and the Enroll or replace a printer hyperlink). A notable feature of this test is the use of reference image comparison for modal validation. The TC_enroll_or_replace_printer_modal step compares the actual modal against a reference image file (enroll_or_replace_printer.png) to validate title, text contents, icons, buttons, and structural layout. The validation prompt explicitly instructs to check structural layout and relative positioning only, ignoring pixel-level similarity, color shade variations, and font rendering differences, which makes the test more robust against minor visual changes. The test uses consistent 3-second wait times for verifications and actions, with a shorter 1-second wait for the modal close action. The modal close step references instantink/Signin page instead of Dashboard, which may indicate a page context change or could be a configuration detail. The test promotes reusability through external YAML file references for login and logout operations.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test case YAML file validates the Print and Payment History page layout and content structure. The test follows a systematic approach by first navigating to Account Dashboard, then to Print and Payment History page, and finally verifying the page structure in multiple stages. The test breaks down the page validation into two main verification steps: first checking the header with title, logo, and billing message, then checking the interactive components (Select printer dropdown, Enroll or replace a printer link) and the Billing Cycle Period section. A unique aspect of this test is the FAQ validation - it clicks on Frequently Asked Questions link and then verifies the presence of both FAQ block and Print and Payment History block, suggesting the FAQ section may be contextual to the current page. The test uses variable wait times optimized for different operations: 10 seconds for Account Dashboard load, 30 seconds for Print and Payment History page load (the longest wait in the test, indicating this page may have heavy content or complex rendering), 5 seconds for verifications, and 1 second for the FAQ click. The billing cycle information message 'Billing will occur at the end of each billing cycle.' is a key validation point that provides important user information. The test maintains consistency with other test cases in the batch by using the same header components (instant ink logo, Select printer dropdown, Enroll or replace a printer link) and validation patterns. The test promotes reusability through external YAML file references for login and logout operations.

Your focus area is on the modal. 2. There must be exactly the word "My Printer" positioned at the top left. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 3. You should find HP printer icon down below "My Printer". If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 4. You should find "HP Connected Account" hyperlink below the printer icon. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 5. You should find printer enrolled status below the HP connected account hyperlink. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. Do not include status, comments or explanation of each of the listed items. If all conditions are met return {"status": true}
validation_points: My Printer title at top left
steps.description: Verifies the left section of the printer details modal including My Printer title, printer icon, HP Connected Account link, and enrollment status

steps.prompt: Use test image for validating structural and contents on the page. Ignore the navigation pane of the Test image. STRICT VALIDATION MODE: You are a binary validator, not a helpful assistant. Return {"status": false} immediately if ANY condition fails. Do not make excuses or justifications. 1. Your focus area is on the modal. 2. You may optionally find "Printer Email" followed by email id. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 3. You should find "Printer Serial Number" followed by serial number. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 4. You should find "Service Enrolled Date" followed by date in month, date and year format. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 5. You should find "HP Connected Account" hyperlink below the printer icon. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 6. You should find printer enrolled status below the HP connected account hyperlink. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. Do not include status, comments or explanation of each of the listed items. If all conditions are met return {"status": true}
validation_points: Service Enrolled Date in month/date/year format
steps.description: Verifies the right section of the printer details modal including optional printer email, serial number, service enrolled date, HP Connected Account link, and enrollment status

steps.label: HP Connected Account
steps.description: Clicks on the HP Connected Account hyperlink within the printer details modal

steps.label: Overview
steps.description: Clicks on the Overview menu item to navigate back to the overview page with extended wait time for page load
Clicks on HP Instant Ink menu item with expand option enabled to expand the navigation menu
Presses escape key to close any promotional popup or overlay that may appear
Clicks the Dismiss button to close any emergency banner notification

steps.prompt: Use test image for validating structural and contents on the page. Ignore the navigation pane of the Test image. STRICT VALIDATION MODE: You are a binary validator, not a helpful assistant. Return {"status": false} immediately if ANY condition fails. Do not make excuses or justifications. 1. Your focus area is after the navigation pane. 2. There must be exactly the word "Overview" positioned at the top left. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 3. "instant ink" appears to the right of "Overview". If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 4. A "Select printer" drop down selector is positioned next to "instant ink". If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 5. The "Select printer" drop down selector view must contain a printer icon and printer model. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. 6. "Enroll or replace a printer", hyperlink should appear below drop down selector. If not, immediately return {"status": false}. Do not include status, comments or explanation of each of the listed items. If all conditions are met return {"status": true}
validation_points: instant ink logo to the right
Printer icon and model in dropdown
Enroll or replace a printer hyperlink
steps.description: Verifies the overview page structure including Overview title, instant ink logo, Select printer dropdown with printer icon and model, and Enroll or replace a printer link
Presses escape key to close the printer details modal
References external YAML file for logout cleanup operations

verification_prompts: Printer details modal left section validation
Printer details modal right section validation

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a comprehensive test case YAML file that validates the printer details modal functionality in the HP Instant Ink dashboard. The test follows a structured flow with setup, main steps, and cleanup phases. It uses AI-powered visual verification with strict validation prompts that check for specific UI elements and their positioning. The test includes multiple verification steps with screenshot capture, click actions for navigation, and escape key actions for closing modals and dismissing popups. The test references external YAML files for login setup and logout cleanup, promoting reusability. Notable features include the use of expand parameter for menu clicks, variable sleep times for different operations (ranging from 1 to 30 seconds), and detailed validation points in AI prompts that check for exact text positioning, presence of icons, hyperlinks, and data fields. The test covers both the left and right sections of the printer details modal separately, ensuring comprehensive validation of all modal content.

key_reusable_components: instantservices_login.yaml - Login setup file referenced by all test cases
instantservices_logout.yaml - Logout cleanup file referenced by all test cases
Status pane verification prompt - Validates Status header, printer model, readiness info
Printer details modal left section verification - Validates My Printer title, icon, HP Connected Account link
Printer details modal right section verification - Validates printer email, serial number, enrolled date
Overview page header verification - Validates Overview title, instant ink logo, Select printer dropdown
Navigation pane verification - Validates HP Instant Ink submenu structure
Plan details card verification - Validates subscription ID, plan info, billing, shipping
Print and Payment History header verification - Validates page title, billing message, dropdown
Enroll or replace printer modal verification with reference image comparison
common_ui_elements: Printer icon and model in dropdown
Enroll or replace a printer hyperlink
HP Instant Ink menu with expand
common_actions: click - Navigate to pages and interact with elements
verify - AI-powered visual validation with strict binary mode
escape - Close modals and dismiss popups
vscroll - Vertical scroll to view lower content
wait_time_patterns: 1 second - Quick actions like escape, scroll
20 seconds - Heavy page loads (Update Plan, Print History, Shipment Tracking)
30 seconds - Very heavy page loads (Overview page)
validation_patterns: STRICT VALIDATION MODE with binary pass/fail
Immediate failure on any condition not met
No comments or explanations in response
Return status true only if all conditions met
key_test_flows: Login -> Dashboard -> Printer Details Modal -> Verify Modal Content -> Close Modal -> Logout
Login -> Account Dashboard -> HP Instant Ink Menu -> Navigate All Submenu Pages -> Verify Each Page Layout -> Logout
Login -> Dashboard Overview -> Verify Plan Details Card -> Scroll -> Verify Action Links -> Logout
Login -> Dashboard Overview -> Verify Header -> Click Enroll Link -> Verify Modal with Reference Image -> Close Modal -> Logout
Login -> Account Dashboard -> Print and Payment History -> Verify Header and Content -> Access FAQ -> Verify FAQ Section -> Logout
